My commitment to mentoring peers reflects my passion for knowledge sharing and growth. Beyond work, I find joy in maintaining my Kubernetes-based home lab and creating memorable moments with my wife and kids.

person.name: Jinhyeok Hong
person.bio: I'm a DevOps engineer in Korea, specializing in Kubernetes, AWS infrastructure, and cloud-native technologies. I primarily work on Go projects and am committed to following best practices. I share my technical insights and experiences on my blog at https://ghdwlsgur.github.io.

person.name: Karol Szymanowski
person.bio: I am a Senior Software Engineer specializing in TypeScript, AWS, and Kubernetes. My work revolves around designing scalable and secure web applications. Passionate about DevSecOps, I thrive on automating processes and I am a strong advocate for "self-driving" infrastructure.

person.name: Mohamed Yassine Elj
person.bio: I’m a DevOps & Cloud Engineer with extensive experience in the fintech sector., deeply passionate about cloud-native technologies and  CNCF ecosystem.  My expertise includes leveraging CI/CD pipelines, DevSecOps practices, and GitOps. My strength lies in adopting an everything-as-code approach to create scalable, robust, and reproducible environments. I thrive on optimizing workflows, leveraging cutting-edge technologies to enhance operational efficiency, streamline deployments, and drive continuous innovation.

person.name: Nicola Noviello
person.bio: I am a Cloud Native enthusiast, Adjunct Professor at the University of Molise, and organizer of two communities: GDG Campobasso and Cloud Native Molise. I specialize in CNCF technologies, Kubernetes, Infrastructure as Code, and DevSecOps. I also work on AI-driven solutions, including LangChain-based applications. Passionate about knowledge sharing, I organize meetups and workshops to help developers and businesses adopt modern cloud-native architectures.

person.name: Alexandr Gumeniuc
person.bio: I'm a passionate cloud and automation engineer, DevOps enthusiast focused on maximizing automation through code. I started working with Kubernetes back in 2020 and absolutely loved it. My working stack is not limited to Kubernetes, other CNCF projects that I am using are: Helm, Argo, Kyverno. I prefer spending free time cycling, hiking or doing other outdoor activities.

person.name: Premkumar Selvaraj
person.bio: Premkumar Selvaraj is a Senior Enterprise DevOps Engineer at Elekta with a strong passion for continuous learning and admire problem solving. He is committed to enhancing his expertise in software development and driving efficiency throughout the SDLC. He advocates for automation through self-service pipelines, empowering stakeholders to navigate technical challenges while improving overall efficiency. Specialized in Azure, Cloud Infrastructure provisioning, management & monitoring, DevSecOps, Microservices architecture, GitOps, and Cybersecurity.

person.name: Sai Pisey
person.bio: I am a DevOps and Cloud Engineer specializing in Kubernetes, Docker, and Terraform, with experience deploying secure and scalable applications across AWS, Azure, and Oracle Cloud. I love automating everything that can be scripted and optimizing what others overlook. A tech-savvy problem solver at heart, I turn complex cloud challenges into clean, efficient solutions.

person.name: Sergej Alikov
person.bio: Sergej is a DevSecOps/Platform Engineer with background in cloud infrastructure, software development, automation, observability, system administration, computer network design and security.

person.name: Vytautas Cirvinskas
person.bio: I’m a Senior… something — DevOps, SRE, Platform, Infrastructure Engineer — honestly, the title keeps changing faster than the tech stack. I work with cloud-native platforms across AWS and Azure, mainly focusing on Kubernetes (EKS and AKS) and infrastructure automation. Most of my time goes into making systems more reliable, scalable, and easier for teams to use, usually with Terraform and solid DevOps practices. I enjoy solving real production problems, from debugging tricky issues to improving platform stability. I’m passionate about cloud-native technologies and continuously learning by applying CNCF tools in real-world environments.

person.name: Yoojung Huh
person.bio: I am a developer specializing in cloud native technologies and modern platform engineering, with hands-on experience across the CNCF ecosystem, including Kubernetes, Prometheus, Argo CD, and container-based infrastructure. My career has evolved from backend development to DevOps and DataOps platform engineering. My work centers on helping teams build reliable, observable, and scalable platforms using practical DevOps and SRE approaches.

person.name: Alexander Gouveia
person.bio: Alexander Gouveia specializes in maintaining and deploying infrastructure for physical security applications worldwide. He has a deep passion for Kubernetes and emerging technologies from the Cloud Native Computing Foundation (CNCF), and he possesses an insatiable thirst for knowledge. His goal is to innovate and deliver efficient, cost-effective solutions for businesses through scalable technologies.

person.name: Christopher Tineo
person.bio: I’m a Cloud Native enthusiast and a firm believer in the value this ecosystem brings to the software industry in LATAM. I lead the Santo Domingo chapter, where we’re bridging the gap between Cloud Native and Open Source technologies in the Dominican Republic. Through sharing my journey and knowledge on LinkedIn and community events

person.name: Daniel Mccracken
person.bio: I am a DevOps and business professional with over two decades of experience spanning enterprise healthcare IT, application development, CI/CD, and Kubernetes administration. I helped found the Google Developers Group of Grand Rapids, MI, which sparked my interest in containerization and orchestration at scale. In 2019, I left the corporate world to help start DevsOperative, Inc. with my friends. They introduced me to Kubernetes around the time of the Kubernetes 1.0 launch, which inspired me to learn and invest in this amazing platform and community. My favorite tools are Helm, ArgoCD, and Cert Manager. Reach out to me on LinkedIn and checkout our stream on Twitch or YouTube called the Cloud Native Coffee Hour!

person.name: Fadi Sohail Jamous
person.bio: I am a Kubernetes and cloud-native engineer specializing in Kubernetes security and platform operations, with hands-on experience across Kubernetes and Red Hat OpenShift. As a Kubestronaut, I have deep knowledge of CNCF technologies including cluster security, networking, observability, and secure application delivery. I enjoy exploring real-world security challenges, improving platform reliability, and helping organizations adopt Kubernetes best practices. I am committed to continuous learning and contributing to the cloud-native community.

person.name: Adam Österberg
person.bio: I am a Platform Engineer in Stockholm, Sweden, using cloud native technology and modern software engineering principles to build reliable, maintainable systems that are a joy to work with.

person.name: Aleksandr Varlamov
person.bio: With 20 years of experience in IT, telecom, and consulting, Aleksandr specializes in designing and delivering complex solutions and services at scale. He provides management and technical leadership to engineering and development teams, with a focus on Kubernetes, Cloud, and Security. His expertise also includes DevOps, GitOps, and DevSecOps.

person.name: Ayoub Ed-Dafali
person.bio: I am a Senior Cloud Native Engineer, with 6+ years of experience.  I love working with Kubernetes, Linux, IaC, Cloud, CI/CD and Python. I am always excited to dive into new innovations, and I love sharing my knowledge with the Open Source community.

person.name: Emmanuel Mendoza Sosa
person.bio: Emmanuel is a seasoned DevOps and Site Reliability Engineer with over a decade of experience across finance, retail, media and supply chain industries. He specializes in Cloud, Infrastructure as Code and Kubernetes, with a strong passion for collaborating with development teams to solve and debug complex problems. Emmanuel likes to learn open-source technologies that enhance and contribute to the DevOps ecosystem.
